stories.title: CPSC Warning — Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets (Strangulation + Fall Hazard)
stories.angle: CPSC warns parents to immediately stop using Tuyedoqe travel bassinets — strangulation risk from restraints + fall hazard from sides too low to contain infant. ~250 units sold on Amazon April–October 2025 for ~$40. Seller (Chinese company) refused to recall. Action: stop using, remove foam pad, cut cover in half, dispose. Do not sell or give away.

article.headline: Stop Using It Now: CPSC Warning on Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Cites Strangulation and Fall Risks

research.facts:
CPSC Warning — Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets
**Sources:**
- cpsc.gov — "CPSC Warns Consumers to Immediately Stop Using Tuyedoqe Travel Bassinets Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Strangulation and Fall Hazards" (official CPSC warning page)
- x.com/USCPSC — CPSC official tweet confirming warning
- threads.com/@uscpsc — CPSC Threads post with additional seller refusal detail
- facebook.com/USCPSC — CPSC Facebook post
- safeinfantsleepalliance Facebook — sharing the warning
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Product: Tuyedoqe travel bassinets — foam sleeping pad + mesh canopy with three toy bears attached [cpsc.gov — primary source]
- Hazards: (1) restraints = strangulation hazard; (2) sides too low = fall hazard; (3) no stand = fall hazard on elevated surfaces [cpsc.gov]
- About 250 units recalled [cpsc.gov]
- Sold on Amazon.com from April 2025 through October 2025 for about $40 [cpsc.gov]
- CPSC issued Notice of Violation to Tuyedoqe (China company) [cpsc.gov + threads CPSC]
- Tuyedoqe has NOT agreed to recall or offer remedy [cpsc.gov + threads CPSC — 2 sources]
- Consumer action: immediately stop using; remove foam pad; cut cover in half; dispose; do NOT sell or give away [cpsc.gov]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- This is an emergency CPSC "Warning" not a formal recall — because seller refused to cooperate [inferred from cpsc.gov warning vs. recall distinction]

stories.title: New AAP Screen Time Guidelines — Major 2026 Shift
stories.angle: The American Academy of Pediatrics has released its first major update to screen time guidance in a decade — and the headline is surprising: no more fixed 2-hour daily screen time limit. The new focus is on quality of digital content and "digital ecosystems" rather than minutes per day. Algorithmic/commercial content flagged as harmful; child-centered design can support development.

article.headline: The 2-Hour Screen Time Rule Is Gone — Here's What the AAP Says Instead

research.facts:
AAP Screen Time Guidelines Update
**Sources:**
- aap.org — "Understanding the New AAP Digital Media Guidelines" (official AAP page)
- publications.aap.org — "Digital Ecosystems, Children, and Adolescents: Policy Statement" (peer-reviewed Pediatrics, vol. 157, Feb 2026)
- edsurge.com — "New AAP 'Screen Time' Recommendations Focus Less on Screens, More on Family Time" (Feb 5, 2026)
- health.choc.org — "Updated AAP recommendations for screen time: What parents need to know"
- medicalxpress.com — FSU psychologist guidance on new recommendations (Feb 2026)
- newcanaanpediatrics.com — "Understanding the Updated AAP Screen and Media Guidelines"
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- AAP released new digital media guidelines in February 2026 (publications.aap.org — primary source)
- No longer recommends a fixed 2-hour daily screen time limit — shift from 2016 policy (edsurge.com + aap.org — 2 sources)
- New focus: quality of content and "digital ecosystems" vs. quantity of time [aap.org]
- Algorithmic/commercial-heavy content can displace healthy behaviors (sleep, movement) and cause negative outcomes [aap.org]
- Child-centered digital design that fosters critical thinking and age-appropriate content can support development [aap.org]
- Policy statement title: "Digital Ecosystems, Children, and Adolescents" in Pediatrics journal [publications.aap.org]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Old guideline: 2 hours/day screen time limit (from 2016) — well-established, multiple sources confirm
- Context: Children now exist within "digital ecosystems" of social media, apps, games, video, AI — not just passive screen time [aap.org]

stories.title: Family Weekend — Winnie the Pooh Interactive Play at Fountain Creek TODAY
stories.angle: Theatre Across Borders is performing an interactive Winnie the Pooh play TODAY (April 11) at 10:30 AM at Fountain Creek Nature Center. $10 per person, ages 2 and under free. Includes craft, 30-minute performance, and a nature hike with the characters. Next performance: April 18 at same location, same time.

research.facts:
Winnie the Pooh Interactive Play at Fountain Creek
**Sources:**

- communityservices.elpasoco.com — Fountain Creek Nature Center official page

- Tickets: $10 per person; ages 2 and under are free [koaa.com]

stories.title: Baby Bath Seat Recall Wave — Multiple Brands, Drowning Risk
stories.angle: CPSC has issued a wave of baby bath seat recalls from Amazon third-party sellers: YCXXKJ (sold by BenTalk) and NFSVLB — both for tip-over + entrapment risks that violate mandatory infant bath seat standards. Multiple models affected. Parents should check under the bath and in the cabinet for these products.

article.headline: Check Your Baby's Bath Seat: Multiple Amazon Brands Recalled for Drowning Risk in 2026

research.facts:
Baby Bath Seat Recall Wave
**Sources:**
- cpsc.gov — "YCXXKJ Baby Bath Seats Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death to Children from Drowning; Violate Mandatory Standard for Infant Bath Seats; Sold on Amazon by BenTalk" (official recall)
- cpsc.gov — "NFSVLB Baby Bath Seats Recalled" (official recall — recall date January 8, 2026)
- cpsc.gov — Baby Bathtub Seats and Rings category page
- consumerreports.org — "Baby Product Recalls Parents Should Know About in 2026"
- myparistexas.com — NFSVLB recall coverage (wire story)
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- NFSVLB bath seats: About 1,430 units; model ZY2025; sold Amazon May–October 2025 for $25–$40; recall date January 8, 2026; hazard: tips over, leg opening allows entrapment [cpsc.gov — primary source]
- NFSVLB action: stop using; write "Recalled" in marker; disassemble; cut suction cups; send photo to NFSVLB; email amzmaihuayd@163.com [cpsc.gov]
- YCXXKJ bath seats: sold by BenTalk on Amazon; recall for same tipping/entrapment violations [cpsc.gov — primary source]
- Both violations: violate mandatory standard for infant bath seats (ASTM F1967) [cpsc.gov]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Wave of similar recalls hitting Amazon third-party bath seat sellers in early 2026 (multiple listings on cpsc.gov confirm this is a pattern) [cpsc.gov category page]

stories.title: Spring Outdoor Guide for COS Families — April Is the Sweet Spot
stories.angle: April in Colorado Springs averages 62°F highs with 10+ hours of sunshine per day — but also has ~6 snowy days. An active weather weekend is possible. COS families have world-class outdoor options: Garden of the Gods (free), Bear Creek and Fountain Creek nature centers, Cheyenne Mountain Zoo, and 200+ miles of trails. A practical family spring outdoor guide.

article.headline: April Outdoor Picks for COS Families: Your Spring Guide to Colorado Springs Parks and Nature

research.facts:
COS Spring Outdoor Guide
**Sources:**
- world-weather.info — Colorado Springs April 2026 weather data
- en.climate-data.org — Colorado Springs April climate averages
- visitcos.com — "Spring Events 2026 in Colorado Springs"
- nationaltoday.com / coloradospringstoday — "Stormy Weather Ahead for Colorado Springs" April 8, 2026
- communityservices.elpasoco.com — Bear Creek and Fountain Creek nature centers
- visitcos.com — "10 for 10 Under" family activities guide
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- April in Colorado Springs: average high ~62°F (17°C); average ~10.1 hours of sunshine daily [climate-data.org + world-weather.info — 2 sources]
- April has approximately 6 snowy days on average [world-weather.info]
- "Active weather weekend" reported for COS around April 8–10, 2026 [nationaltoday.com/coloradospringstoday]
- Garden of the Gods: free to visit [visitcos.com established fact]
- Bear Creek Nature Center and Fountain Creek Nature Center: El Paso County Parks managed [communityservices.elpasoco.com]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- "200+ miles of trails" in COS area [visitcos.com — approximate figure commonly cited]
- COS has 100+ parks [city of Colorado Springs parks — general figure, widely cited]
